title: Microsoft adopts DeepSeek: the China-commoditisation shock
question: When Microsoft begins testing a fine-tuned DeepSeek model as a low-cost alternative inside Copilot, has the China-commoditisation shock reached the center of the Western AI stack - and is it a margin event or a demand event for the market?
finding: Yes, and it presents as a MARGIN-repricing event, not a demand-destruction event - AI usage keeps rising (Copilot seats >20M) while model-layer pricing power erodes. The cost wedge is real, large and structural: DeepSeek V4 Pro lists ~11-34x cheaper than GPT-5.5 and ~57x cheaper than Anthropic (launched $0.10/$0.30 per M tokens, -71/-73% vs V3); Chinese open-weight share went ~1% -> ~15% of global usage in a year, weekly tokens briefly overtaking the US; DeepSeek raised $7.4B at ~$50B (Tencent/CATL) to keep funding the price war; US enterprises already switching (DeepSeek #1 on Ramp's corporate-spend panel, June 2026). Market impact: BULLISH Microsoft (strips third-party model margin out of cost, keeps OpenAI equity upside), BEARISH OpenAI (loses distribution exclusivity vs its $38.5B 2025 net loss) / Anthropic (the 57x is measured vs it) / Western API pricing power, MIXED Nvidia (volume up via Jevons but premium margin already cracking 75->71% GM), tail-BEARISH the debt-funded neoclouds. CAPEX-FINANCING TABLE (SEC-sourced, 2025-26, ordered by money raised): Amazon ~$81.7B debt (+59% capex/+12% rev), Alphabet $64.6B debt (+74%/+15%; NOT an equity raise - it bought back $45.7B stock), Oracle $43B debt (+209%/+8%, FCF now negative), Meta $30B bond + ~$27B off-BS Hyperion JV (+87%/+22%), CoreWeave ~$13.3B junk-rate debt+IPO, Nebius ~$11.65B, Dell ~$8.5B (no AI earmark, capex falling), Applied Digital ~$4.5B earmarked (single tenant=CoreWeave); MICROSOFT raised NOTHING - self-funds (OCF $136B > capex $64.6B), debt falling 4 years, and is ACCELERATING capex (FY26 9mo +69%, ~$190B CY26 guided) - the 'Microsoft cuts capex / first domino' narrative is a disputed Feb-2025 analyst channel-check REFUTED by the filings; the China risk shows up at MSFT in metered Copilot pricing and off-balance-sheet leverage, not capex cuts. The ROI risk concentrates in the debt-funded builders where capex badly outruns revenue (Oracle, CoreWeave, Applied Digital), not at self-funded Microsoft. A multi-agent simulation of the shock splits ~65/35 toward margin-repricing across 5 scenarios; the single deciding variable is DEMAND ELASTICITY - does cheaper inference create enough new paid usage to offset a 57x cost cut (demand-expansion event) or not (margin-repricing event). Caveats: list-vs-effective price, subsidy-funded cuts (re-prices, not a proven permanent moat - the study-30 point), panel data, usage-share != value-share. Ties studies 27 (named the path) + 30 (named the mechanism) -> 31 is the event.
